title: Dungeon Degenerates: The Hand of Doom - customizing the game for sandbox play
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vy88MX2ss_g

Daniel (@dungeondive) addresses the mission structure that prevents him from playing *Hand of Doom* as often as he'd like, then demonstrates how to hack it into a sandbox experience using cards already in the box. He walks through creating a custom monster-hunting mode with the Demonic Void as the final boss, tracking clues across the four regions, and managing threat without rigid campaign structure. Using Mel Lee's solo RPG guide and Eric Basherd's zine expansions, Daniel reveals how phenomenally tight the game's engine truly is—you can strip away the campaign missions entirely and still get hours of grungy, emergent adventure. He explores different ways to seed encounters, generate NPCs using built-in oracles, establish victory conditions flexibly, and leverage the degree-of-success dice mechanic for narrative weight. This deep dive demonstrates why Dungeon Degenerates attracts long-term players: the rules-as-written foundation is strong enough to support countless customizations while maintaining the game's grimy soul and anarchic spirit.

----

Have you modified *Hand of Doom* to play more as a sandbox, or stuck with the campaign structure? What would your ideal 'quick play' version look like?
